text: Writing clean code is essential for any developer who wants to create software that is easy to understand and modify. Readable code is like a well-written book—it tells a clear story of what the program does. One of the best ways to achieve this is by using good naming conventions. Instead of naming a variable "x," call it "userAge" so its purpose is obvious. Similarly, functions should do one thing well and have descriptive names like "calculateTotalPrice" rather than "doSomething." By following these practices, your code becomes more maintainable, meaning other developers (or yourself in the future) can read, fix, and improve it without confusion.
